title: Server-side requests should not be vulnerable to traversing attacks
description: This rule detects and prevents vulnerabilities in server-side requests that could allow attackers to perform directory traversal or similar attacks, potentially exposing sensitive files or resources.
recommendation: Validate and sanitize all user-supplied input used in server-side requests. Avoid using input directly in file paths or resource locators. Employ security libraries or frameworks that mitigate traversal attacks.
impact: Exploitation of this vulnerability can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive files, data leakage, or system compromise.

examples.bad: String file = request.getParameter("file"); FileInputStream fis = new FileInputStream("/var/data/" + file);
examples.good: String file = sanitize(request.getParameter("file")); FileInputStream fis = new FileInputStream("/var/data/" + file); // 'sanitize' ensures no traversal

checks.message: Validate and sanitize all user-supplied input used in server-side requests. Avoid using input directly in file paths or resource locators. Employ security libraries or frameworks that mitigate traversal attacks.
